    <td class="d-block color-fg-default comment-body markdown-body js-comment-body">
        <div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-warning" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-alert mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M6.457 1.047c.659-1.234 2.427-1.234 3.086 0l6.082 11.378A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.082 15H1.918a1.75 1.75 0 0 1-1.543-2.575Zm1.763.707a.25.25 0 0 0-.44 0L1.698 13.132a.25.25 0 0 0 .22.368h12.164a.25.25 0 0 0 .22-.368Zm.53 3.996v2.5a.75.75 0 0 1-1.5 0v-2.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0ZM9 11a1 1 0 1 1-2 0 1 1 0 0 1 2 0Z"></path></svg>Warning</p><p dir="auto">I have not tested these commands.</p>

<p dir="auto">A couple recommendations:</p>
<h1 dir="auto">[Performance] Disable transition animation scales, improving device performance by reducing animation overhead.</h1>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="adb shell settings put global transition_animation_scale 0"><pre class="notranslate"><code class="notranslate">adb shell settings put global transition_animation_scale 0
</code></pre></div>
<h1 dir="auto">[Security] Disable 2G connectivity, protecting the device against <a href="https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/android-users-can-now-disable-2g-to-block-stingray-attacks/" rel="nofollow">Stingrays</a> aka IMSI Catchers.</h1>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="adb shell settings put global preferred_network_mode 9"><pre class="notranslate"><code class="notranslate">adb shell settings put global preferred_network_mode 9
</code></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">The <code class="notranslate">preferred_network_mode</code> table (<a href="https://cs.android.com/android/platform/superproject/main/+/main:packages/services/Telephony/src/com/android/phone/settings/RadioInfo.java;l=129;drc=de40a3f44e01829e821bebb49047b4332c176b9b" rel="nofollow">source</a>):</p>

};"><pre class="notranslate"><span class="pl-k">private</span> <span class="pl-k">static</span> <span class="pl-k">final</span> <span class="pl-smi">String</span>[] <span class="pl-c1">PREFERRED_NETWORK_LABELS</span> = {

        <span class="pl-s">"NR only"</span>,
        <span class="pl-s">"NR/LTE"</span>,
        <span class="pl-s">"NR/LTE/CDMA/EvDo"</span>,
        <span class="pl-s">"NR/LTE/GSM/WCDMA"</span>,
        <span class="pl-s">"NR/LTE/CDMA/EvDo/GSM/WCDMA"</span>,
        <span class="pl-s">"NR/LTE/WCDMA"</span>,
        <span class="pl-s">"NR/LTE/TDSCDMA"</span>,
        <span class="pl-s">"NR/LTE/TDSCDMA/GSM"</span>,
        <span class="pl-s">"NR/LTE/TDSCDMA/WCDMA"</span>,
        <span class="pl-s">"NR/LTE/TDSCDMA/GSM/WCDMA"</span>,
        <span class="pl-s">"NR/LTE/TDSCDMA/CDMA/EvDo/GSM/WCDMA"</span>,
        <span class="pl-s">"Unknown"</span>
};</pre></div>
<p dir="auto">By setting the preferred network mode to a value that excludes 2G (GSM) networks, you effectively disable 2G communications.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code class="notranslate">GSM</code> = 2G</li>
<li><code class="notranslate">WCDMA</code>, <code class="notranslate">CDMA</code>, <code class="notranslate">EvDo</code>  = 3G</li>
<li><code class="notranslate">TDSCDMA</code> = <a href="https://www.rfwireless-world.com/Tutorials/TD-SCDMA-tutorial.html" rel="nofollow">Chinese 3G</a></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">LTE</code> = 4G</li>
<li><code class="notranslate">NR</code> = 5G NR</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-warning" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-alert mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M6.457 1.047c.659-1.234 2.427-1.234 3.086 0l6.082 11.378A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.082 15H1.918a1.75 1.75 0 0 1-1.543-2.575Zm1.763.707a.25.25 0 0 0-.44 0L1.698 13.132a.25.25 0 0 0 .22.368h12.164a.25.25 0 0 0 .22-.368Zm.53 3.996v2.5a.75.75 0 0 1-1.5 0v-2.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0ZM9 11a1 1 0 1 1-2 0 1 1 0 0 1 2 0Z"></path></svg>Warning</p><p dir="auto">The <code class="notranslate">PREFERRED_NETWORK_LABELS</code> varies across the different Android versions. Code 9 in Android 8 is not the same as code 9 in Android 14.</p>
</div>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-note" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-info mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M0 8a8 8 0 1 1 16 0A8 8 0 0 1 0 8Zm8-6.5a6.5 6.5 0 1 0 0 13 6.5 6.5 0 0 0 0-13ZM6.5 7.75A.75.75 0 0 1 7.25 7h1a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v2.75h.25a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-2a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.5h.25v-2h-.25a.75.75 0 0 1-.75-.75ZM8 6a1 1 0 1 1 0-2 1 1 0 0 1 0 2Z"></path></svg>Note</p><p dir="auto">This protects the device against Stingrays, but <a href="https://www.cloudwards.net/how-to-block-stingray-surveillance/" rel="nofollow">Hailstorms</a> are still effective.</p>
</div>
<h1 dir="auto">[Privacy] Enable Private DNS with AdGuard:</h1>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="adb shell settings put global private_dns_mode hostname
adb shell settings put global private_dns_specifier dns.adguard-dns.com"><pre class="notranslate"><code class="notranslate">adb shell settings put global private_dns_mode hostname
adb shell settings put global private_dns_specifier dns.adguard-dns.com
</code></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">Using private DNS allows DNS queries to be sent in a non-vulnerable-to-espionage way to the DNS resolver. Using the AdGuard servers, also enables users to block ads.</p>
    </td>
  </tr>

  </table>

    <td class="d-block color-fg-default comment-body markdown-body js-comment-body tmp-px-3 pt-0 pb-2">
        <p dir="auto">I like the private DNS there are also alternative servers to adguard blocking trackers and ads e.g.<br>
adblock.dns.mullvad.net (info: <a href="https://mullvad.net/en/help/dns-over-https-and-dns-over-tls" rel="nofollow">https://mullvad.net/en/help/dns-over-https-and-dns-over-tls</a>)<br>
dnsforge.de (info: <a href="https://dnsforge.de/" rel="nofollow">https://dnsforge.de/</a>)<br>
but I think having also script option with a server/servers which don't block (maximum compatibility), but also doesn't log requests and doesn't filter them is also good: e.g.<br>
unfiltered.adguard-dns.com (info: <a href="https://adguard-dns.io/de/public-dns.html" rel="nofollow">https://adguard-dns.io/de/public-dns.html</a>)<br>
dns.mullvad.net (info: <a href="https://mullvad.net/en/help/dns-over-https-and-dns-over-tls" rel="nofollow">https://mullvad.net/en/help/dns-over-https-and-dns-over-tls</a>)<br>
dns3.digitalcourage.de (info: <a href="https://digitalcourage.de/support/zensurfreier-dns-server" rel="nofollow">https://digitalcourage.de/support/zensurfreier-dns-server</a>)</p>
<p dir="auto">All of the above is related to DNS over TLS (DoT) which often causes problems in company networks, where internal names cannot be resolved then.</p>
<p dir="auto">with information from <a href="https://www.kuketz-blog.de/empfehlungsecke/#dns" rel="nofollow">https://www.kuketz-blog.de/empfehlungsecke/#dns</a> (in German)</p>
    </td>
  </tr>

    </tbody>
  </table>

        <p dir="auto">Warning: I have not tested these commands.</p>
<h1 dir="auto">Android 12: Getting the old WiFi toggle back under Quick Settings</h1>
<p dir="auto">Since Android 12, the WiFi quick toggle button has been changed. A tap no longer activates or deactivates the WiFi interface; instead, a selection of different network connections is displayed. Only setting the slider changes the connectivity - but it can be adjusted via ADB:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="adb shell 'settings put global settings_provider_model false'
adb shell 'settings put secure sysui_qs_tiles "wifi,cell,$(settings get secure sysui_qs_tiles)"'"><pre class="notranslate"><code class="notranslate">adb shell 'settings put global settings_provider_model false'
adb shell 'settings put secure sysui_qs_tiles "wifi,cell,$(settings get secure sysui_qs_tiles)"'
</code></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">Reversion code:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="adb shell 'settings put global settings_provider_model true'
adb shell 'settings put secure sysui_qs_tiles "internet,$(settings get secure sysui_qs_tiles)"'"><pre class="notranslate"><code class="notranslate">adb shell 'settings put global settings_provider_model true'
adb shell 'settings put secure sysui_qs_tiles "internet,$(settings get secure sysui_qs_tiles)"'
</code></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">Translated with DeepL.com (free version) from <a href="https://www.kuketz-blog.de/android-12-alten-wifi-toggle-unter-quick-settings-wiederbekommen/" rel="nofollow">https://www.kuketz-blog.de/android-12-alten-wifi-toggle-unter-quick-settings-wiederbekommen/</a></p>
    </td>
  </tr>

    </tbody>
  </table>

  <table class="d-block" role="presentation" data-paste-markdown-skip="">
    <tbody class="d-block js-translation-source" data-target-translation-id="14204370" data-target-translation-type="comment">
        <tr class="d-block">
    <td class="d-block color-fg-default comment-body markdown-body js-comment-body">
        <div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-note" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-info mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M0 8a8 8 0 1 1 16 0A8 8 0 0 1 0 8Zm8-6.5a6.5 6.5 0 1 0 0 13 6.5 6.5 0 0 0 0-13ZM6.5 7.75A.75.75 0 0 1 7.25 7h1a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v2.75h.25a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-2a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.5h.25v-2h-.25a.75.75 0 0 1-.75-.75ZM8 6a1 1 0 1 1 0-2 1 1 0 0 1 0 2Z"></path></svg>Note</p><p dir="auto">Some of these commands were tested on Samsung devices. They focus on disabling or uninstalling non-essential bloat apps via ADB for priva debloating.</p>
</div>
<h3 dir="auto">Remove Bloatware (Galaxy S10 / Android 12 style)</h3>
<p dir="auto">These packages were primarily tested on Samsung devices running Android 12.</p>
<p dir="auto">To restore an uninstalled package, you can use this command:</p>
<p dir="auto"><code class="notranslate">adb shell cmd package install-existing <package_name></code><br>
(Replace <package_name> with the full package name found in the previous step, e.g., com.google.android.apps.photos).</p>
<h4 dir="auto">General / System</h4>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.android.bookmarkprovider</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.android.dreams.basic</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.android.dreams.phototable</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.android.egg</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.android.wallpaper.livepicker</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.android.providers.partnerbookmarks</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.android.vpndialogs</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.sec.android.easyonehand</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.sec.android.daemonapp</code></li>
</ul>
<h4 dir="auto">Samsung / OEM Apps</h4>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.sec.android.app.shealth</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.arzone</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.video</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.sec.android.app.samsungapps</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.dynamiclock</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.tvplus</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.lool</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.messaging</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.oneconnect</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.voc</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.calendar</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.app.voicenote</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.app.dressroom</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.scloud</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.sdk.handwriting</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.universalswitch</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.safetyinformation</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.storyservice</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.service.aircommand</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.app.aodservice</code></li>
</ul>
<h4 dir="auto">Samsung Pass / Pay</h4>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.samsungpassautofill</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.samsungpass</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.spay</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.spayfw</code></li>
</ul>
<h4 dir="auto">AR / Stickers</h4>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.aremoji</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.sec.android.mimage.avatarstickers</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.aremojieditor</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.stickercenter</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.app.camera.sticker.facearavatar.preload</code></li>
</ul>
<h4 dir="auto">Facebook</h4>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.facebook.system</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.facebook.appmanager</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.facebook.services</code></li>
</ul>
<h4 dir="auto">Printing</h4>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.android.bips</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.google.android.printservice.recommendation</code></li>
</ul>
<h4 dir="auto">Samsung Games</h4>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.game.gametools</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.game.gos</code></li>
</ul>
<h4 dir="auto">Edge / DeX</h4>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.service.peoplestripe</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.samsung.android.app.appsedge</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.sec.android.dexsystemui</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.sec.android.desktopmode.uiservice</code></li>
<li><code class="notranslate">pm uninstall -k --user 0 com.sec.android.app.desktoplauncher</code></li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">You can also put <code class="notranslate">disable</code> instead which is a safer method</p>
<p dir="auto">source: <a href="url">https://xdaforums.com/t/galaxy-s10-s10-debloat-bloatware-removal-list.3912073/</a></p>
    </td>
  </tr>

    </tbody>
  </table>
